title: Crucible Health: inference latency spike + transient 5xx on /v1/generate (INC-9821)
preview: Customer escalation relayed by Stephanie Nguyen. Crucible Health reported sudden latency spike and burst of 5xx on inference endpoint /v1/generate beginning ~2026-08-28 07:55 PDT. Reported impact: median latency increased from ~120ms to ~900ms; throughput dropped from ~250 req/s to ~150 req/s for Crucible production...
text:
Customer escalation relayed by Stephanie Nguyen. Crucible Health reported sudden latency spike and burst of 5xx on inference endpoint /v1/generate beginning ~2026-08-28 07:55 PDT. Reported impact: median latency increased from ~120ms to ~900ms; throughput dropped from ~250 req/s to ~150 req/s for Crucible production key; initial customer-observed 5xx rate ~8% during the window; multiple batches timed out and downstream throttles triggered. Customer had a critical demo at 10:00 PDT.

Immediate hypotheses (serving): autoscaler oscillation under burst + degraded single shard causing head-of-line blocking.

Immediate mitigations executed: emergency scale-up of dedicated pool (target +20% capacity) + temporary concurrency limit increase + drain suspected bad instance(s) + enable routing of new requests to compatible fallback model variant (lower context) to reduce per-token work.

Outcome ...

title: Containment & fast-snapshot restore for tenant optimizer surge causing tail latency spikes
preview:
Issue summary:
BrightCart reported a sudden increase in 95th/99th percentile token latency beginning 2026-03-12 09:10 UTC after an automated optimizer profile rollout. The change coincided with enabling aggressive batching + prefix-prefetch + an int8 quantization profile for several tenant routes.

text:
Issue summary:
BrightCart reported a sudden increase in 95th/99th percentile token latency beginning 2026-03-12 09:10 UTC after an automated optimizer profile rollout. The change coincided with enabling aggressive batching + prefix-prefetch + an int8 quantization profile for several tenant routes.

Impact:
- Multiple customer application endpoints experienced 95/99p token-latency spikes from ~120ms to 900-1200ms.
- Elevated request queueing and ~2% 5xx spike observed for BrightCart tenant routes during the window.
- Affects revenue-critical checkout flow (routes pinned to dedicated pool).

Environment:
- Dedicated capacity pool: dc-dedicated-09 (us-east-1)
- Tenant: brightcart-prod (dedicated)
- Models: rw-3.0-q4_0 (primary), rw-7b-int8 (fallback)
- Runtime version: 2026.02.28-release
- Optimizer profile applied automatically via policy: opt-profile-2026-03-12-agg-v2

Steps to reprodu...

title: Per-tenant delivery receipts, watermarking, and compliance evidence crosswalk request
preview:
Issue summary: Customer BrightHealth Analytics requests a bundled compliance artifact that demonstrates per-tenant ordered delivery, per-event delivery receipts, persistent watermarking (monotonic event sequence), and a crosswalk mapping between the audit fields we provide and the customer SIEM schema.

Impact: Cust...
text:
Issue summary: Customer BrightHealth Analytics requests a bundled compliance artifact that demonstrates per-tenant ordered delivery, per-event delivery receipts, persistent watermarking (monotonic event sequence), and a crosswalk mapping between the audit fields we provide and the customer SIEM schema.

Impact: Customer needs this artifact to complete a data residency + SIEM compliance package for their SOC review. Without the bundle they cannot finalize onboarding of a regulated workload, delaying an enterprise contract.

Environment: Production (us-east-1). Customer is on enterprise plan with private ingestion endpoints and uses our tenant-scoped downstream webhooks into their Splunk HEC cluster.

Request details: BrightHealth expects: (1) a per-tenant manifest showing monotonic watermarks and last-applied offsets for the period 2026-02-01 to 2026-02-28, (2) delivery-receipt logs fo...

title: Sudden latency and tail-time spike in dedicated pool following tenant preemption/eviction events
preview: Issue summary: Since ~2026-03-10T14:30Z, NimbleAI has reported a sharp increase in median and p99 latency for requests routed to their dedicated GPU pool. The spike correlates with a series of preemptive eviction events and rapid tenant slot reassignments triggered by autoscaler activity. Impact: Customer-facing API...
text: Issue summary: Since ~2026-03-10T14:30Z, NimbleAI has reported a sharp increase in median and p99 latency for requests routed to their dedicated GPU pool. The spike correlates with a series of preemptive eviction events and rapid tenant slot reassignments triggered by autoscaler activity. Impact: Customer-facing API calls experienced 5–12x higher p99 tail latency for ~40 minutes; several streaming sessions disconnected mid-stream. This is impacting production throughput and user-facing SLAs. Environment: Dedicated pool (dedicated-az-usw2-03), prod traffic, region us-west-2. Steps observed/repro: 1) High short-lived burst from tenant A caused scheduler to preempt idle slots and reassign GPUs. 2) Eviction sequence triggered slot draining and KV-cache invalidations. 3) Tenants B and C received sudden allocation changes causing KV cache misses and rewarm overhead. 4) Requests experienced ...

title: Request: add/assign 'Quality Gate Override Approver' role for Optimize time-bound overrides (Console RBAC)
preview:
Request type: Access / RBAC change

Background:
Optimize Quality Gates are now blocking Apply when a gate fails. We have a time-bound override flow (admin-controlled) for documented exceptions (see exception policy). During limited production rollout, we need a designated oncall/PM to be able to approve overrides in...
text:
Request type: Access / RBAC change

Background:
Optimize Quality Gates are now blocking Apply when a gate fails. We have a time-bound override flow (admin-controlled) for documented exceptions (see exception policy). During limited production rollout, we need a designated oncall/PM to be able to approve overrides in Console without using a shared admin account.

What is needed:
1) Create (or confirm) a dedicated RBAC role/group for this capability:
 - Role name (proposal): quality_gate_override_approver
 - Group name (proposal): optimize-quality-gate-override-approvers
2) Permissions required (least privilege):
 - Approve/deny time-bound override requests for Optimize config changes (batching/caching/quant profile)
 - View gate run details + artifacts links sufficient to make decision
 - View audit log entries related to overrides (read-only)
 - No ability to edit gating thresholds, d...

text:
Request type: Break-glass (emergency admin elevation)

Requester: Colin O'Donnell (Solutions Eng, Private customer pilot lead)
Requested by (ticket reporter): Fiona McCarthy (SecOps)
System(s): Redwood Private Control Plane (customer VPC), RBAC v2 enabled (pilot)
Customer context: Northbridge Healthcare (Private POC)

Reason / business justification:
- During a scheduled POC validation window, Northbridge admins were blocked from exporting audit logs and rotating an API key due to a mis-scoped role binding created during initial bootstrap.
- Customer impact: POC validation blocked; risk of missing security sign-off milestone.
- Standard path (customer admin fixes via Console) not possible because the affected binding controlled Console admin access.

Scope of requested access:
- Minimal actions required:
